description: Notarize-only record of an inbound dispute as the Stripe/issuer System of Record reported it. AGLedger does NOT adjudicate the dispute (the issuer/Visa network renders won/lost upstream; Stripe relays it) — this record attests, signed and hash-chained, what was asserted and when the response clock started. Mirrors the fields on Stripe's `charge.dispute.created` webhook. | `evidenceDueBy` is the real, externally-imposed deadline (Stripe `evidence_details.due_by`); downstream gated decisions should carry it so a missed window is observable on-chain. | `reason` is Stripe's raw network reason; `category` is the evidence-guidance bucket the merchant maps it to (kept separate because the two enums are not 1:1 and the merchant's mapping is itself an attestable choice). Pure notarization: reaches terminal RECORDED in one round-trip.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...; older docs show dp_). The SoR's primary key for this dispute.

chargeRef.description: The disputed charge (ch_...) or PaymentIntent (pi_...) the dispute is attached to.

reason.description: Stripe's raw network dispute reason (Dispute.reason), verbatim enum.

disputeCategory.description: Evidence-guidance category the merchant maps the raw reason to; drives which evidence fields the submission decision requires. (Named disputeCategory, not category — `category` is a reserved record-metadata field name.)

amount.description: Disputed amount in major currency units (e.g. 249.99). Funds are pulled from balance immediately and held for the dispute's duration.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code, lowercase (Stripe convention), e.g. usd.

evidenceDueBy.description: Hard external deadline to submit evidence (Stripe evidence_details.due_by). due_by absent / in the past means no response is accepted.

network.description: Card network the dispute rides on (payment_method_details.card.brand). Inquiries (warning_*) arise on multiple networks — Amex/Discover inquiries and Visa pre-dispute (RDR) among them; don't assume a network from the inquiry flow (the Stripe test harness produces a Visa inquiry).

networkReasonCode.description: Network reason code where surfaced, from payment_method_details.card.network_reason_code, e.g. Visa 10.4 (fraud) or 13.1 (merchandise not received).

vcrClass.description: Visa Claims Resolution workflow class derived from the reason code: allocation (fraud 10.x / authorization 11.x — liability auto-assigned by the network) vs collaboration (processing 12.x / consumer 13.x — evidence exchange). Stripe ABSTRACTS the VCR workflow (no distinct merchant action per class; no pre-arbitration/arbitration — unsupported), so this is a captured classification for audit/routing, not a Stripe-driven state. `unknown` when no Visa code is surfaced.

disputeFlow.description: Whether this filing is an inquiry (Stripe warning_* status — softer, pre-chargeback, resolvable by refund with no dispute fee) or a formal chargeback (needs_response). Determines which gate shape applies: inquiry-resolution-decision-v1 vs evidence-submission-decision-v1.

isChargeRefundable.description: Whether the underlying charge can still be refunded (false once a formal chargeback is open; true for an inquiry or early-fraud-warning pre-dispute signal — the refund-to-prevent path).

stripeStatus.description: Stripe Dispute.status at the time of notarization.
